privacy.title: Privacy Policy | AJSEE
privacy.description: How AJSEE processes personal data, uses cookies and analytics tools, and what rights you have.
privacy.ogDescription: How AJSEE processes personal data, uses cookies and analytics tools, and what rights you have.

hero.title: Privacy Policy
hero.lead: On this page, you can find information about what personal data AJSEE may process, why we process it, how long we keep it, and what rights you have.

toc.title: Contents
toc.purposes: How and why we use data

intro.title: 1. Introduction
intro.p1: AJSEE helps users discover events and related travel or leisure services through trusted partners. When operating our website, we may process personal data, for example when you contact us, subscribe to updates, send a business enquiry, or interact with optional analytics and referral technologies.
intro.p2: We aim to process personal data transparently, securely, and only to the extent necessary for a given purpose and in line with applicable data protection laws, including the UK GDPR and, where relevant, the EU GDPR.

controller.title: 2. Controller details
controller.p1: Unless stated otherwise for a specific form or service, the controller of the personal data described on this page is AJSEE LTD.

data.title: 3. What data we may process
data.p1: Depending on how you use our website or contact us, we may process the following categories of personal data in particular:

items.1: identification and contact data, such as your name, email address, company name, and message content;
items.2: newsletter-related data, such as your email address and subscription preferences;
items.3: data included in a business or partner enquiry that you voluntarily send to us;
items.4: technical and usage data, such as IP address, browser and device information, visited pages, approximate location, traffic source, and on-site interactions, where analytics cookies are accepted;
items.5: limited data related to partner click-through or referral attribution when you move from AJSEE to a third-party website.

data.p2: At present, we do not offer user account registration on the website and we do not intentionally collect special categories of personal data through standard forms.

purposes.title: 4. How and why we use data

1.title: Handling enquiries
1.text: We use contact details and message content to respond to your enquiries, handle support requests, and communicate about services you have shown interest in.
1.basis: legitimate interests and, where relevant, steps taken at your request before entering into a contract.

2.title: Newsletter and updates
2.text: If you subscribe to our newsletter or similar updates, we use your email address to send the requested content and manage your subscription preferences.

3.title: Website analytics and service improvement
3.text: We use analytics data to understand traffic, improve performance, measure content effectiveness, and make the website clearer and easier to use.
3.basis: consent for optional analytics technologies; legitimate interests for basic website security and troubleshooting.

4.title: Partnership and business communication
4.text: If you contact us about cooperation or partnership opportunities, we process the data you provide in order to assess and manage a potential business relationship.
4.basis: legitimate interests and, where relevant, pre-contractual steps.

5.title: Legal obligations and protection of claims
5.text: We may retain or use relevant records where necessary to comply with legal obligations, keep records, protect our rights, or defend legal claims.

purposes.p1: We do not sell your personal data.

cookies.title: 5. Cookies and analytics
cookies.p1: Our website may use cookies and similar technologies. Some are necessary for website functionality, security, or basic preferences. Others are optional and are used only where you consent through the cookie banner or your preferences settings.

items.1: necessary technologies required for the proper and secure operation of the website;
items.2: Google Tag Manager for managing tags and consent-dependent integrations;
items.3: Google Analytics, where enabled, to understand aggregated website usage and improve the service;
items.4: partner or referral tracking parameters when you move from AJSEE to selected third parties.

cookies.p2: You can manage optional cookies through the cookie banner or your browser settings. Disabling some cookies may affect certain non-essential functions or measurement capabilities.

sharing.title: 6. Sharing and processors
sharing.p1: We may share personal data with trusted service providers and processors who help us operate the website, communications, analytics, hosting, or other necessary business functions. Where these providers act as processors, they may process personal data only on our instructions.
sharing.p2: If you click through to a partner website, such as a ticketing or accommodation partner, that third party becomes independently responsible for the data you provide on its website. We therefore recommend reviewing the privacy information of the relevant third party as well.

transfers.title: 7. International transfers
transfers.p1: Some service providers or partners may process data outside your country of residence. Where international transfers of personal data take place, we aim to rely on appropriate safeguards, such as adequacy decisions or standard contractual clauses, where required by law.

retention.title: 8. Retention periods
retention.p1: We keep personal data only for as long as necessary for the relevant purpose, taking into account legal, operational, and evidentiary needs. Specific periods may vary depending on the type of interaction.

items.1: contact or support enquiries: usually for the time needed to handle the request and for a reasonable period afterwards;
items.2: newsletter data: until you unsubscribe or withdraw consent, unless a longer period is required by law;
items.3: analytics data: according to the retention settings of the relevant analytics tools;
items.4: business records: for as long as necessary to manage cooperation, comply with legal obligations, or protect legal claims.

rights.title: 9. Your rights
rights.p1: Depending on applicable law and your place of residence, you may have the right to request access to your personal data, rectification, erasure, restriction of processing, portability, and to object to certain processing. Where processing is based on consent, you may withdraw that consent at any time without affecting the lawfulness of earlier processing.
rights.p2: If you want to exercise your rights, please contact us using the details below. We may ask you to verify your identity before handling your request.

contact.title: 10. Contact and complaints
contact.p1: For privacy-related questions or requests, you can contact us at adam@ajsee.cz.
contact.p2: You may also have the right to lodge a complaint with the relevant data protection supervisory authority in your country or with the authority relevant to the controller.

changes.title: 11. Changes to this policy
changes.p1: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time to reflect legal, technical, or business changes. The latest version will always be published on this page together with the updated date.
